message:
fix(freshness): address Copilot review comments on #6239 (#6244) (#6249)

ResourceUsage, ClusterFocus, and GPUNamespaceAllocations had freshness
indicators that didn't accurately reflect all data sources used by each
card. Copilot's review of #6239 caught three issues:

* ResourceUsage uses both useClusters() and the GPU node cache, but
  the freshness indicator only used gpuLastRefresh, so cluster metric
  staleness was hidden. Now uses Math.min(clustersLastRefresh, gpuLastRefresh).

* GPUNamespaceAllocations used a nested ternary with truthy comparisons
  (`gpuLastRefresh && podsLastRefresh`) which would have treated a `0`
  epoch timestamp as missing. Replaced with explicit `typeof === 'number'`
  checks in an IIFE.

* ClusterFocus reads health and metrics from useClusters() but its
  freshness indicator only considered GPU/pod/deployment caches, so a
  stale cluster cache could appear fresh. Now includes
  clustersLastRefresh in the Math.min set and clustersRefreshing in
  the isRefreshing flag.

All three now use `typeof === 'number'` filters so a `0` timestamp
is preserved as the epoch (still extremely stale, but represented
truthfully) rather than silently dropped.

Closes #6244

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
